title: Isolation and Identification of Cancer Stem Cells by ALDH Activity Assay
author: Kwang Nickel
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bfyrjpv6

steps.instruction: Prepare activated ALDEFLUOR assay reagent, Bodipy-aminoacetaldehyde (BAAA), according to the manufacturer’s directions ALDEFLUOR assay kit manual.pdf . Make 50 µL aliquots and store them at -20 °C . Note: In this example, a head and neck cancer cell line, UM-SCC47 SCC071CA.pdf , is analyzed. The experimental approach can be used to study cells isolated from primary human cancers or with established cancer cell lines. Also, Attune NxT (Thermo Fisher Scientific) is used as a choice of flow cytometer.

Trypsinize UM-SCC47 cells and count cells using a hemocytometer or automated cell counter. Cell numbers should be at least 3x 10 6 to prepare the following 6 tubes. Unstained PI only Test (ALDH) Control (DEAB) Test (ALDH) without PI: FITC only Control (DEAB) without PI: FITC only

Centrifuge cells at 250 x g, °C, for 5 min to pellet, remove the supernatant and resuspend in ALDEFLUOR assay buffer to the final concentration of 1 x10 6 cell/ml in the both “Test” tubes. Prepare each Unstained and PI tube with 0.5 X 10 6 cells with 0.5 ml ALDEFLUOR assay buffer.

Add 5 µL of diethylaminobenzaldehyde (DEAB), reagent provided as part of the ALDEFLUOR assay kit to both “Control” tubes. Close the tube immediately to prevent the evaporation of the DEAB solution (95% ethanol).
Add 5 µL of activated ALDEFLUOR reagent to both “Test” tubes and mix by gentle vortexing or inversion.
Immediately transfer 0.5 mL of mixture from each ”Test” tube to each “Control” tube.
Incubate both “Test” and “Control” tubes for 45 min at 37 °C in the dark. Do not exceed 60 min (incubation time should be optimized for cell type).
Following incubation, gently pellet cells by centrifugation at 250 x g, °C, for 5 min.
Remove supernatant and resuspend cells in 0.5 mL of ALDEFLUOR assay buffer. Transfer to 5 ml flow tubes and store the cells on ice or at 4 °C in the dark.
Add 1 µL of 1 mg/ml PI to PI, “Test “and “Control” tubes prior to flow analysis. Do not add PI to “Test” and “Control” FITC only tubes.
Create an Experiment. On the main menu, click New Experiment. Select Experiment type and enter Experiment name.

Compensation Compensation setup is needed due to the spillover of FITC into PI caused by overlapping spectrum of FITC (ALDH positive cells will be detected in this channel) to PI. Click Compensation Setup and select the source of compensation controls (BL1 for FTIC and BL2 for PI), the measurement parameter, background fluorescence mode and compensation parameters. Optimize FSC (Forward Scatter) and SSC (Side Scatter) Voltage and Threshold settings for Unstained sample. Compensation for Unstained sample Optimize Fluorescence channel settings for FITC and PI with FITC and PI only samples. Compensation for FITC Compensation for PI Record compensation.

Run sample. Set up for 5 dot plots. dot plot 1: SSC-H (Height) vs. SSC-A (Area), to eliminate doublets dot plot 2: FSC-H vs. FSC-A, to eliminate doublets (second discrimination) dot plot 3: SSC-H vs. FSC-H, to select single cells dot plot 4: SSC-H vs. PI-H, to detect only live single cells dot plot 5: SSC-H vs. ALDH-H, to select ALDH+ cells Run “Control (DEAB)” sample to set the gate of the ALDH positive population using the rightmost edge of the stained the “Control” DEAB population. This permits to identify the negative cells with no ALDH activity (ALDH-) and the positive cells with ALDH activity (ALDH+).Place the corresponding ALDH “Test” tube into the flow cytometer. Collect 100,000 events in live single cells gate. UM-SCC47 control (DEAB) UM-SCC47 Test (ALDH)

In this figure, A: the Gate is set on the DEAB treated sample in order to include a small number of cells as ALDH positive (4.14%). B: a representative plot for UM-SCC47 cells is shown and demonstrates 39.09% of the population is ALDH-positive. Percentage of ALDH+ CSCs in UM-SCC47 cells We typically perform a minimum of 3 biologic replicates (3 independent experiments) and average the present positive cells from the 3 replicates. When this approach is taken, the mean and standard deviation should be reported.
